examples.text: إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَظْلِمُ مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ وَإِنْ تَكُ حَسَنَةً يُضَاعِفْهَا وَيُؤْتِ مِنْ لَدُنْهُ أَجْرًا عَظِيمًا
examples.english: Indeed, Allah does not do injustice, as much as an atom's weight; while if there is a good deed, He multiplies it and gives from Himself a great reward.

senses.gloss: mithqal, a certain weight measure in the Arab world of varying value, usually around 4.5 grams

etymology.text: Tool noun derived from ثَقُلَ (ṯaqula, “to be heavy”).
